phase3_findings.description: Stale ordinal count 'a judgment row like the other three' at regenerate-artifacts.sh:270 — the diff registered a fifth judgment row and edited the hunk directly above it, so the count rotted inside the edited hunk (non-demotable stale-count class).

is_budget_row docstring carries a self-referential exact count ('today's two command-less rows ... the two budget rows'); nothing in the suite goes RED on drift. Corroborated by Phase 0.6 stale-prose-lint R3 count-locked row at the same site.
The disjointness assertion renders a green 'disjoint' result for a comparison it never performed when both lists are empty — unknown rendered as the pass value.

Phase 3.1.5: the A4 watch-list audit enumerated the budget-row population from hardcoded call sites while the registry is that population's declared single enumeration point; a third registered budget row would receive no equality assertion and no overlap comparison, with every arm staying green. Independent re-enumeration from the registry (ROWS + is_budget_row) matched today, so nothing was uncovered — but coverage was not structurally maintained.
The new row's budget-watch-missing attribution leg is driven by no arm (A5e/A5i drive review-bundle-budget only).
A6e lacks the sibling-clean anti-vacuity pin A6d carries; its rc-0 assertion carries no independent signal.
is_budget_row's documented divergence from the argv-is-None proxy is asserted by no test.
RA_WATCH_LAST is an implicit out-parameter with no staleness guard; a reordered or inserted call site would bind the wrong row's list.
The replacement for the deleted BUDGET_* constants gives no pointer to is_budget_row, the predicate that decides which rows carry those keys.
Header INCLUSION CRITERION still requires 'a standalone non-writing check command'; budget rows have none, and this change doubles the contradicting population.
AC7 (full suite, zero skip tally) unverified within the review pass itself.

fix_decisions.claim_text: AC7 unverified in review — only the focused module was run
fix_decisions.evidence: The full suite WAS run in-env on the committed tree before the review pass and observed 10767 passed, 0 failed, with a zero skip tally; recorded in the workpad. The reviewer could not observe that read-only, so the finding is about reviewer visibility rather than a gap in the change.

parking_evidence.basis: the claimed gap does not exist — the full-suite zero-skip run was performed in-env and recorded before the review pass
parking_evidence.failing_input: none; the asserted missing verification was in fact performed
parking_evidence.source: workpad note on issue #624 recording 10767 passed, 0 failed, zero skips on the committed tree
